steps.title: Read circle A's radius from its equation
steps.content: The standard form $(x-h)^2 + (y-k)^2 = r^2$ tells us that $r_A^2 = 25$, so $r_A = 5$. The center is $(3, 9)$.

steps.title: Double the radius and square the result
steps.content: Circle B keeps the same center $(3, 9)$ but has radius $r_B = 2 \cdot 5 = 10$, so $r_B^2 = 100$. Its equation is $(x-3)^2 + (y-9)^2 = 100$.

steps.title: Check each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — $(x-3)^{2}+(y-9)^{2}=625$: This gives $r^2=625$, so $r=25$, which is five times circle A's radius, not twice. ✗
  • Choice B — $(x-3)^{2}+(y-9)^{2}=250$: This gives $r=\sqrt{250} \approx 15.8$, not $10$. ✗
  • Choice C — $(x-3)^{2}+(y-9)^{2}=50$: This gives $r=\sqrt{50} \approx 7.07$, not $10$. ✗
  • Choice D — $(x-3)^{2}+(y-9)^{2}=100$: This gives $r=10$, exactly twice circle A's radius. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
